channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Mali defence minister killed amid flurry of insurgent attacks
item.description: Mali's defence minister Sadio Camara was killed in a car bomb attack on his residence. Insurgent groups including al-Qaida's West African affiliate staged coordinated assaults.

item.title: Record arms spending, again
item.description: Global military spending reached a record high in 2025, according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute. Global military spending hit a record high in 2025. The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) warns the trend is likely to continue.

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals merge in bid to oust his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's rivals merged to oust his government. Two opposition groups joined forces to challenge his coalition. Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals have announced they are joining forces to attempt to oust his coalition government.

item.title: Defence experts warn of fitness tracker risks in Singapore military bases amid global Strava
item.description: Singapore's Ministry of Defence is aware of fitness tracker risks in military bases. Security measures will be implemented as needed. The Ministry of Defence says it is aware of the risks and will implement security measures when needed.

item.title: Singapore Airlines’ Indian ambition has an Air India problem
item.description: Air India asked Singapore Airlines for financial support, posing a challenge to Singapore Airlines' Indian expansion plans. The Indian airline has reportedly asked Singapore Airlines for financial support. Shareholders will need some convincing, says former financial journalist Ven Sreenivasan.

item.title: A daily wait for ad hoc work - and a window into a labour market under pressure
item.description: China has thousands of state-backed gig stations, linking workers to low-paid manual work, many of whom are older and shut out of stable jobs. Thousands of state-backed “gig stations” have sprung up across China, linking workers - many older and shut out of stable jobs - to low-paid manual work.

item.title: Working from home isn’t the flexibility that Singapore families need from employers
item.description: Victor Seah says flexi-time arrangements matter more for Singapore employees with parenting needs, not working from home. It’s flexi-time arrangements that matter more for employees with parenting and caregiving needs, says Victor Seah of the Singapore University of Social Sciences.

item.title: Opinion | Why US-led security alliances in Asia are losing coherence
item.description: US-led security alliances in Asia are losing coherence according to the South China Morning Post. Opinion | Why US-led security alliances in Asia are losing coherence South China Morning Post.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon as Israel warns residents to leave towns beyond 'buffer zone'
item.description: Israeli strikes in south Lebanon killed 14 people after the ceasefire with Hazbollah failed. Strikes have resumed in south Lebanon as the ceasefire between Israel and Hazbollah failed. Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon as Israel warns residents to leave towns beyond 'buffer zone'.

item.title: Milan and Juve offer up another 0-0 draw to consolidate top-four grip
item.description: Milan and Juventus drew 0-0 to maintain their top-four positions. Milan and Juve offer up another 0-0 draw to consolidate top-four grip.

item.title: Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks
item.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi travelled to Russia for talks with senior officials. Abbas Araghchi travels to Russia, where he will speak with "senior officials", Iran's foreign ministry says. Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks.

item.title: On the U.S. and India’s independent foreign policy
item.description: The US is limiting India's ability to pursue an independent foreign policy. The U. S. is slicing away at India’s ability to pursue an independent foreign policy. On the U. S. and India’s independent foreign policy.

item.title: Former Israeli PMs Bennett, Lapid unite to challenge Netanyahu in elections
item.description: Former Israeli PMs Bennett and Lapid formed an alliance to challenge Netanyahu in elections. The two politicians' alliance is aimed at uniting a fragmented opposition against current PM Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: King Charles' visit to US to proceed after Washington shooting
item.description: King Charles' visit to the US will proceed as planned despite a shooting in Washington. The Royal Family is cooperating with the US Secret Service to ensure security during the visit.

item.title: Hindu temple caretaker found dead in Bangladesh, minority group demands strong action
item.description: A Hindu temple caretaker was found dead in Bangladesh, three days after going missing. The body was recovered in Cox's Bazar, and a minority group is demanding strong action against those responsible.

item.title: Israel issues forced evacuation orders for southern Lebanon in escalation
item.description: Israel issued forced evacuation orders for southern Lebanon amid ceasefire tensions. Hezbollah rejects allegations from Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu that it is undermining the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Israel issues forced evacuation orders for southern Lebanon in escalation.

item.title: Death toll from bus bombing in southwest Colombia rises to 20 during a wave of violence
item.description: The death toll from a bus bombing in southwest Colombia has risen to 20. The device was set off while the bus was traveling on the Panamerican Highway in Cajibio, according to Governor Octavio Guzman.

item.title: Trump says U.S., Iranian officials can talk by phone
item.description: Donald Trump said US and Iranian officials can communicate by phone. He also expressed disappointment with NATO, suggesting the US may consider leaving the military alliance. Asked about NATO, Mr.

item.title: Trump Event Shooting Highlights: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials
item.description: Donald Trump was evacuated after gunshots were heard at his event. The accused shooter, identified as Allen from Torrance, California, was taken into custody by police. Trump Event Shooting Highlights: Accused Shooter Likely Targeted Trump And His Top Officials.

item.title: 10 Minutes Before Firing At Trump Event, Shooter Sent A Manifesto To Family
item.description: Allen sent his manifesto to his family 10 minutes before firing at the Trump event. His brother raised the alarm after receiving the manifesto. It was Allen's brother who first raised the alarm.
